conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
Sorry to hear that. What's been making things feel slow for you?
Just everything, I guess. Work's been a grind, and nothing seems to be going right.
Can you tell me more about what's not going right at work?
Honestly, just feeling stuck in this warehouse job. It's like I'm just a cog in the machine or something.
That sounds frustrating. Have you talked to anyone about how you're feeling?
Yeah, my boss is always telling me to stay positive and focus on the task at hand, but it doesn't really help.
So, it's like they're not taking your feelings into consideration. How do you handle that kind of feedback?
I just shrug it off and try to move on. Don't want to rock the boat or anything.
Okay. Can you tell me more about what you mean by "don't want to rock the boat"?
Just don't want to cause any problems or make a fuss. Everyone's always telling me to calm down and not get too worked up, like I'm being dramatic or something.
It sounds like there might be some pressure to conform to certain expectations. How do you think your relationships with others are affected by this?
Honestly, they're all pretty superficial. People just kind of drift in and out of my life – no real connections.
I see. Can you tell me more about what you mean by "drift in and out"?
Just that people come into my life for a little bit, and then they move on to something else. No real commitment or anything.
So, it sounds like you might be having trouble finding meaningful connections with others. What do you think is getting in the way of that?
Maybe I'm just not putting myself out there enough? I don't know – it's all pretty confusing to me.
It can be tough to figure things out on your own. Have you tried seeking out any new activities or hobbies to help take your mind off things?
Not really, no. Just been trying to get by, day-to-day.
Okay. Sometimes taking a break and doing something we enjoy can help us feel more grounded. What do you think might be a source of enjoyment for you?
Honestly, I don't know what that looks like right now – everything feels pretty bleak.
That's okay. We can explore some ideas together. Can you tell me about your family and friends? Do they offer any support or understanding?
Yeah, my mom is always trying to help, but sometimes her advice feels kinda intrusive, you know?
I see. It sounds like she might be a bit overbearing at times. Are there any other people in your life who offer a listening ear?
Uh, no – I don't really have anyone else.
Okay. It can be tough to find people who understand what we're going through. What do you think is the most challenging part of this experience for you so far?
Just feeling like I'm stuck in this rut and don't know how to get out – or even if it's possible.
Okay, that sounds really tough. Sometimes when we feel stuck, it helps to identify what's keeping us there. Can you think of anything specific that might be contributing to your feelings?
Maybe just feeling like I'm not good enough or doing something wrong? I don't know – it all feels pretty bleak.
Okay, let's explore this idea a bit further. What makes you feel like you're not good enough or doing something wrong?
Just everything, really. Work, relationships – nothing seems to be going right.
It sounds like there might be some underlying self-criticism at play here. Can you tell me more about what that's like for you?
Yeah, it's just constant, I guess. Like, no matter how hard I try, I always feel like I'm failing or not doing enough.
Okay, so it sounds like this self-criticism is a major source of distress for you. Have you ever considered talking to someone about these feelings?
Honestly, I don't know – I guess I've been too ashamed or something.
Okay, that takes a lot of courage just to acknowledge those feelings. Can you tell me more about what makes you feel ashamed?
Just feeling like I'm not handling things right – that I'm supposed to be some kind of super-human who can overcome anything.
I see. It sounds like there might be some societal expectations at play here. Can you think of any specific times or situations where this shame has come up for you?
Yeah, definitely when my mom is pushing me to do more – like, she always wants me to be a certain way, and it makes me feel like I'm not meeting her expectations.
Okay, that sounds really tough. It might be helpful to talk about this with your mom sometime – or maybe even set some boundaries for yourself.
Yeah, maybe...I don't know, it's all pretty overwhelming right now.
That makes sense. Sometimes just acknowledging our emotions can feel like a huge burden lifted off our shoulders. What do you think might be a next step for us to work on in this session?
Honestly, I'm not sure – I just want things to get better, I guess.
Okay, that's a great starting point! Let's set some goals for ourselves and come up with a plan to start working towards those. How does that sound?
